items.headline: Tech-Led Rally Continues Amid Geopolitical Focus and Inflation Concerns
items.summary: U.S. equities saw mixed movements with a strong tech-led rally pushing indices to new highs, while investors weighed hotter-than-expected inflation data and ongoing geopolitical tensions. Overall market sentiment improved, remaining in the 'Greed' zone, with particular attention on the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing and its implications for global trade and the Middle East conflict.

items.statContext: The main stock market index rose to 7464 points on May 14, gaining 0.26% from the previous session.
Closed up 1.20% on May 13, at a new record high. Nasdaq 100 futures were up 0.35% in pre-market on May 14.
Closed down 0.14% on May 13. Dow futures were up 0.44% in pre-market on May 14.

items.headline: Cisco Systems Soars on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Cisco Systems Inc. soared in pre-market trading on Thursday after announcing better-than-expected third-quarter financial results and raising its FY26 guidance.

items.headline: Nvidia Continues AI-Driven Gains
items.summary: Chip giant Nvidia advanced, contributing significantly to the S&P 500's gains, as AI-driven momentum continues to fuel technology stocks.

items.headline: Mainz Biomed Leads Penny Stock Gains
items.summary: Mainz Biomed N.V. was among the top gainers on May 14, experiencing a substantial percentage increase.

items.headline: Doximity Plunges in Pre-Market
items.summary: Doximity Inc. plunged in pre-market trading on Thursday.

items.headline: Sugar Stocks Drop on Export Ban
items.summary: Shares of sugar manufacturers, including Dhampur Sugar Mills, dropped on Thursday after the government imposed an immediate ban on sugar exports until September 30 to improve domestic supply.

items.headline: LTM Ltd Hits 52-Week Low
items.summary: LTM Ltd, a large-cap Computers - Software & Consulting player in India, touched an intraday low on May 14, marking a decline amid broader market and sector-specific pressures.

items.headline: Key US Economic Data Releases Today
items.summary: Investors are closely watching today's release of April Retail Sales and Jobless Claims in the US, due at 14:30 CET, which will provide a crucial backdrop for Federal Reserve policy considerations.

items.headline: Inflation Concerns Persist, Fed's Hawkish Stance Reinforced
items.summary: Hotter-than-expected producer prices in April, rising at the fastest pace since 2022, reinforce expectations that the Federal Reserve may maintain a hawkish policy. April wholesale prices jumped 1.4% month-over-month, with headline PPI surging to 6% year-over-year. FOMC members Hammack, Barr, and Williams are scheduled to speak tonight.

items.headline: Major Companies Report Earnings Today
items.summary: Today's earnings reports include Dillard's Inc. ($DDS), Applied Materials Inc. ($AMAT), Legence Corp. ($LGN), and Xcel Brands ($XELB) for Q1 2026. These reports are keenly watched for financial updates and forward guidance.

items.headline: Trump-Xi Summit and Iran Conflict Shape Global Narrative
items.summary: The summit between President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing remains a dominant global narrative, influencing market sentiment amid discussions on trade, tariffs, and the persistent Iran conflict. Elevated crude oil prices continue due to Middle East tensions.

items.headline: Tech and Healthcare Lead, Utilities and Consumer Staples Lag
items.summary: Technology, communication services, and consumer discretionary sectors are showing strength, largely driven by AI optimism. In contrast, utilities and financial stocks lagged on Wednesday, and consumer staples face pressure from inflation, leading to potential sector rotations.
